regions.name: Caribbean Coast
regions.bestTimeToVisit: Visit December to March for the driest weather and best beach conditions.

regions.name: Atlantic Interior
regions.bestTimeToVisit: Visit December to March for warm, dry weather ideal for exploring colonial towns.

regions.name: Santa Marta & Lost City
regions.bestTimeToVisit: Trek December to March or August to September when trails are most passable.

regions.name: Coffee Triangle
regions.bestTimeToVisit: Visit year-round, but April to June and September to November offer the best harvest experiences.

regions.name: Bogotá & Cundinamarca
regions.bestTimeToVisit: Visit December to January or July to August during the drier seasons for sightseeing.

regions.name: Southwest Highlands
regions.bestTimeToVisit: Visit December to March for dry weather and vibrant cultural celebrations.

regions.name: Nariño & Pasto
regions.bestTimeToVisit: Visit June to September or December to January for clearer mountain views and festivals.

regions.name: Amazon
regions.bestTimeToVisit: Visit July to September for lower water levels and easier wildlife viewing.

regions.name: Orinoco Plains
regions.bestTimeToVisit: Visit December to March for the dry season with best wildlife and horseback riding opportunities.

regions.name: Pacific Coast
regions.bestTimeToVisit: Visit July to November for whale watching and calmer weather conditions.

regions.name: Northeastern Highlands
regions.bestTimeToVisit: Visit December to March for clear skies and ideal conditions for hiking and exploring.

top10.name: Bogotá
top10.description: Colombia's capital and largest city, perched at 2,640 meters in the Andes with a rich cultural scene and world-class museums. Home to the Gold Museum, vibrant neighborhoods like La Candelaria, and modern dining and nightlife districts.

top10.name: Cartagena
top10.description: A UNESCO World Heritage walled city with colorful colonial architecture, cobblestone streets, and Caribbean charm. It's the most iconic destination on the coast, offering history, culture, dining, and beach access.

top10.name: Coffee Triangle
top10.description: A UNESCO World Heritage zone covering three departments known for producing world-class coffee surrounded by lush green mountains. Visitors come to tour working coffee plantations, experience agritourism, and enjoy the stunning landscape and hospitality.

top10.name: San Andrés
top10.description: A Caribbean island archipelago famous for its pristine beaches, coral reefs, and water sports opportunities. It is a major destination for tourists seeking tropical island relaxation, diving, snorkeling, and duty-free shopping.

top10.name: Cali
top10.description: Known as the world capital of salsa, this vibrant city pulses with music, dance, and cultural energy. Tourists visit for its salsa clubs and performances, museums, and as a cultural hub for experiencing Colombian music and nightlife.

top10.name: Villa de Leyva
top10.description: A charming colonial mountain town featuring a large main plaza, whitewashed buildings, and surrounding natural attractions. It appeals to tourists seeking a picturesque Andean village atmosphere, outdoor activities, and proximity to archaeological sites.

top10.name: Leticia
top10.description: Colombia's main gateway to the Amazon, located at the tri-border junction with Brazil and Peru on the Amazon River. Essential base for jungle lodges, river tours, and experiencing indigenous cultures and wildlife in the world's largest rainforest.
